premarket.market_expectations: Markets are expected to open lower with a short bias, influenced by negative overnight moves in U.S. indices and a stronger dollar. Rising oil prices and mixed domestic institutional flows add to the cautious tone, though easing volatility may provide some support.

news.headline_reworded: Markets give up intraday advances to close slightly up
news.why_it_matters: This indicates ongoing volatility and a lack of strong bullish momentum despite recent gains.

news.headline_reworded: Benchmarks notch third consecutive rise as volatility recedes
news.why_it_matters: Sustained gains with lower volatility could signal a stabilizing trend in the near term.

news.headline_reworded: Midday surge fueled by sharp drop in volatility index
news.why_it_matters: A significant decline in the VIX suggests reduced market fear, potentially encouraging more participation.

news.headline_reworded: Indices extend winning streak amid focus on quarterly results
news.why_it_matters: Continued upward movement highlights the influence of corporate earnings on market direction.

news.headline_reworded: VA Tech Wabag gains on Bengaluru wastewater project win
news.why_it_matters: Positive stock reaction to new contracts may boost sentiment in the infrastructure sector.
